displayTitle: Make the Call, Then Defend It

beats.narration: You've got the trade-off tables for every penetrant method. Here a Level Two turns them into a selection you can sign.

payload.title: MAKE THE CALL
payload.sub: A real part, a governing spec, a documented choice you can defend.

beats.narration: A complete selection is a classification string — type, method, sensitivity, developer form. The spec fixes part of it; your judgment fills whatever it leaves open.

payload.heading: The selection is a string
payload.note: TYPE · METHOD · SENSITIVITY · DEVELOPER — SPEC CHOOSES FIRST

beats.narration: Run every job through five questions. How fine a flaw, what material and surface, what geometry, how many parts and where, what the site can support.
Watch it work. Titanium fan disks ride Method D for removal control. A daylight weld gets visible dye. Rough castings run water-washable.
And sometimes the defensible call isn't penetrant at all. A clean PT can't clear a flaw that never broke the surface.

payload.sub: Surface-breaking flaw, nonporous part — or it's another method's job.
